subject: [PATCH v2 084/110] hfs: replace PRIino with %llu/%llx format strings

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the use of %llu/%llx format strings in hfs code, specifically that they are not necessary since i_ino is already u64 and PRIino has been removed. The author's response is neutral, explaining their reasoning without committing to a fix.

reviews.author: Viacheslav Dubeyko
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where i_ino is a hexadecimal value, and suggested using %llx format string instead of %llu to avoid potential issues.
reviews.raw_body:
Reviewed-by: Viacheslav Dubeyko <slava@dubeyko.com>

Thanks,
Slava.
______________________________________________________
Linux MTD discussion mailing list
http://lists.infradead.org/mailman/listinfo/linux-mtd/

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch updates the HFS file system to use explicit format strings for printing inode numbers, replacing the PRIino macro which has been removed since inode numbers are now u64 type.
